items.title: Neural network training
items.description: Every SGD, Adam, or AdamW run sets a base learning rate; mis-tuned η is one of the first causes of non-learning or exploding loss in deep models.

items.title: Transfer learning and fine-tuning
items.description: Pretrained models often need a smaller η than training from scratch to avoid destroying useful weights while adapting to a new task.

items.title: Hyperparameter search
items.description: Automated sweeps (grid, random, Bayesian) frequently prioritize η because it strongly affects convergence speed and final loss.

items.title: Classical convex optimization
items.description: Logistic regression, linear models, and SVM solvers expose step-size or learning-rate parameters with similar too-large/too-small tradeoffs on convex objectives.
